title: Organic Acids as Analytical Reagent. Part I : Estimation of Zirconium by Gallic Acid
author: C. S. PANDE
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Chemistry Department, Lucknow University, Lucknow-7 

  Manuscript received 1 March  1975;  accepted 5 April  1975 

 Gallic acid has been found to be a selective reagent for the estimation of zirconium. The acid gives white crystalline precipitate at  \(p\) H of 4.8. The precipitate is ignited and weighed as ZrO 2 . Cations like Ca +2 , Ba +2 , Sr +2 ,Mn +2 , Co +2 , Ni +2 , Fe +3  do not interfere in the estimation.
